---
title: "Driving Test Route Analysis"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import json
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
from pathlib import Path

from road_risk.config import _ROOT as ROOT

# Load routed routes
routes_path = ROOT / "data/processed/test_routes/routed_routes.parquet"
snapped_path = ROOT / "data/processed/test_routes/snapped_routes.parquet"
feats_path = ROOT / "data/features/dtc_route_features.parquet"
risk_path = ROOT / "data/models/risk_scores.parquet"

routes = pd.read_parquet(routes_path) if routes_path.exists() else pd.DataFrame()
feats = pd.read_parquet(feats_path) if feats_path.exists() else pd.DataFrame()
risk = pd.read_parquet(risk_path) if risk_path.exists() else pd.DataFrame()

print(f"Routes     : {len(routes):,} | Centres: {routes['dtc_name'].nunique() if not routes.empty else 0}")
print(f"DTC feats  : {len(feats):,} rows × {feats.shape[1]} cols")
print(f"Risk scores: {len(risk):,} links")
```

## Overview

This page analyses crowdsourced driving test routes from centres across the
study area. Routes are snapped to the OS Open Roads network and scored against
the collision risk model. The goal is to understand whether the road environment
of a test centre's routes is associated with its DVSA pass rates and fault
patterns.

See [Test Route Methodology](../methodology/test-routes.qmd) for details on
how routes are processed and features are computed.

For the DVSA outcome context behind this sub-project, see
[DVSA FOI Test-Centre Findings](dvsa-foi-cracknell.qmd). That page summarises
Jake Cracknell's 2023 FOI analysis and frames why route environment and
centre-level fault patterns are worth linking.

---

## Route coverage {#coverage}

```{python}
if not routes.empty:
    by_centre = routes.groupby("dtc_name").agg(
        n_routes=("file_name", "count"),
        source=("source", lambda x: "/".join(sorted(x.unique()))),
        mean_length_km=("route_length_km", "mean"),
        mean_links=("n_unique_links", "mean"),
    ).sort_values("n_routes", ascending=False).reset_index()

    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, max(4, len(by_centre) * 0.4 + 1)))

    axes[0].barh(by_centre["dtc_name"], by_centre["n_routes"], color="steelblue")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Number of routes")
    axes[0].set_title("Routes per test centre")
    axes[0].invert_yaxis()
    axes[0].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

    axes[1].barh(by_centre["dtc_name"], by_centre["mean_length_km"], color="steelblue")
    axes[1].set_xlabel("Mean route length (km)")
    axes[1].set_title("Mean route length per centre")
    axes[1].invert_yaxis()
    axes[1].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

    print(by_centre[["dtc_name", "n_routes", "source", "mean_length_km", "mean_links"]].to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
if not routes.empty and "route_length_km" in routes.columns:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

    axes[0].hist(routes["route_length_km"].dropna(), bins=20, edgecolor="none", color="steelblue")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Route length (km)")
    axes[0].set_ylabel("Routes")
    axes[0].set_title("Route length distribution")
    axes[0].grid(alpha=0.2)

    if "n_unique_links" in routes.columns:
        axes[1].hist(routes["n_unique_links"].dropna(), bins=20, edgecolor="none", color="steelblue")
        axes[1].set_xlabel("Unique road links")
        axes[1].set_ylabel("Routes")
        axes[1].set_title("Links per route")
        axes[1].grid(alpha=0.2)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

---

## Road environment by centre {#environment}

```{python}
if not feats.empty:
    env_cols = {
        "pct_high_speed": "High speed (≥40 mph)",
        "pct_low_speed": "Low speed (≤30 mph)",
        "pct_roundabout": "Roundabout links",
        "pct_dual": "Dual carriageway",
        "pct_right_turns": "Right turns",
        "pct_left_turns": "Left turns",
    }
    available = {k: v for k, v in env_cols.items() if k in feats.columns}
    n = len(available)

    if n > 0:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, min(n, 3), figsize=(5 * min(n, 3), max(4, len(feats) * 0.35 + 1)))
        if n == 1:
            axes = [axes]

        for ax, (col, label) in zip(axes, list(available.items())[:3]):
            sorted_df = feats.dropna(subset=[col]).sort_values(col, ascending=False)
            ax.barh(sorted_df["dtc_name"], sorted_df[col] * 100, color="steelblue")
            ax.set_xlabel("% of route links")
            ax.set_title(label)
            ax.invert_yaxis()
            ax.grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

        plt.suptitle("Route environment by test centre", y=1.01)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()
```

```{python}
if not feats.empty:
    env_cols2 = [k for k in [
        "pct_roundabout", "pct_dual", "pct_high_speed", "pct_low_speed",
        "pct_right_turns", "pct_left_turns", "right_left_ratio",
        "n_roundabouts", "mean_speed_est",
    ] if k in feats.columns]

    if env_cols2:
        show = feats[["dtc_name", "n_routes"] + env_cols2].copy()
        for c in env_cols2:
            if c.startswith("pct_"):
                show[c] = (show[c] * 100).round(1)
            else:
                show[c] = show[c].round(2)
        show = show.sort_values("n_routes", ascending=False)
        print("Road environment features by centre:\n")
        print(show.to_string(index=False))
```

---

## Risk scores on test routes {#risk}

```{python}
if not feats.empty and "risk_percentile_mean" in feats.columns:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, max(4, len(feats) * 0.4 + 1)))

    sorted_df = feats.dropna(subset=["risk_percentile_mean"]).sort_values(
        "risk_percentile_mean", ascending=False
    )

    axes[0].barh(sorted_df["dtc_name"], sorted_df["risk_percentile_mean"],
                 color="steelblue")
    axes[0].axvline(50, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8,
                    label="Network median (50th)")
    axes[0].set_xlabel("Mean risk percentile")
    axes[0].set_title("Mean link risk percentile by centre")
    axes[0].invert_yaxis()
    axes[0].legend(fontsize=8)
    axes[0].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

    if "pct_high_risk" in feats.columns:
        sorted_df2 = feats.dropna(subset=["pct_high_risk"]).sort_values(
            "pct_high_risk", ascending=False
        )
        axes[1].barh(sorted_df2["dtc_name"], sorted_df2["pct_high_risk"] * 100,
                     color="#e74c3c")
        axes[1].set_xlabel("% of route links")
        axes[1].set_title("% of links in top risk decile (≥90th percentile)")
        axes[1].invert_yaxis()
        axes[1].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

    plt.suptitle("Collision risk exposure on test routes", y=1.01)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

```{python}
if not feats.empty:
    risk_cols = [c for c in [
        "risk_percentile_mean", "risk_percentile_p90", "pct_high_risk",
        "residual_glm_mean", "estimated_aadt_mean",
    ] if c in feats.columns]

    if risk_cols:
        show = feats[["dtc_name", "n_routes"] + risk_cols].copy()
        for c in ["risk_percentile_mean", "risk_percentile_p90"]:
            if c in show:
                show[c] = show[c].round(1)
        if "pct_high_risk" in show:
            show["pct_high_risk"] = (show["pct_high_risk"] * 100).round(1)
        if "residual_glm_mean" in show:
            show["residual_glm_mean"] = show["residual_glm_mean"].round(3)
        if "estimated_aadt_mean" in show:
            show["estimated_aadt_mean"] = show["estimated_aadt_mean"].round(0).astype(int)

        show = show.sort_values("risk_percentile_mean", ascending=False)
        print("Risk scores by centre:\n")
        print(show.to_string(index=False))
```

::: {.callout-note}
Risk percentile reflects historical collision rates on each link relative to
the full network. A centre with a high mean percentile uses roads that, on
average, have higher collision rates than most of the network. This does not
mean those roads are dangerous *during a test* — risk scores are not
conditioned on time of day or weather.
:::

---

## Turn complexity {#turns}

```{python}
turn_cols = [c for c in [
    "pct_right_turns", "pct_left_turns", "pct_straight",
    "right_left_ratio", "n_junctions",
] if not feats.empty and c in feats.columns]

if turn_cols and not feats.empty:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, max(4, len(feats) * 0.4 + 1)))

    # Stacked bar: right / left / straight
    stk_cols = [c for c in ["pct_right_turns", "pct_left_turns", "pct_straight"]
                if c in feats.columns]
    if stk_cols:
        sorted_df = feats.dropna(subset=stk_cols[:1]).sort_values(
            "right_left_ratio" if "right_left_ratio" in feats.columns else stk_cols[0],
            ascending=False
        )
        bottom = np.zeros(len(sorted_df))
        colours = ["#e74c3c", "#3498db", "#2ecc71"]
        labels = ["Right turns", "Left turns", "Straight"]
        for col, colour, label in zip(stk_cols, colours, labels):
            vals = sorted_df[col].fillna(0) * 100
            axes[0].barh(sorted_df["dtc_name"], vals, left=bottom,
                         color=colour, label=label, alpha=0.85)
            bottom += vals.values
        axes[0].set_xlabel("% of junctions")
        axes[0].set_title("Turn composition by centre")
        axes[0].invert_yaxis()
        axes[0].legend(fontsize=8)
        axes[0].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

    # Right/left ratio
    if "right_left_ratio" in feats.columns:
        sorted_rl = feats.dropna(subset=["right_left_ratio"]).sort_values(
            "right_left_ratio", ascending=False
        )
        axes[1].barh(sorted_rl["dtc_name"], sorted_rl["right_left_ratio"],
                     color="steelblue")
        axes[1].axvline(1, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8,
                        label="Equal right/left")
        axes[1].set_xlabel("Right turns ÷ left turns")
        axes[1].set_title("Right/left turn ratio")
        axes[1].invert_yaxis()
        axes[1].legend(fontsize=8)
        axes[1].grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

    plt.suptitle("Turn analysis — bearings from link geometry", y=1.01)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

---

## Correlations with DVSA outcomes {#correlations}

```{python}
corr_path = ROOT / "data/features/dtc_correlations.csv"

if corr_path.exists():
    corr = pd.read_csv(corr_path)
    print(f"Correlation table: {len(corr):,} rows | "
          f"Outcomes: {corr['outcome'].nunique()} | "
          f"Features: {corr['feature'].nunique()}")
else:
    corr = pd.DataFrame()
    print("Correlation file not found — run dtc/analysis.py")
```

### Pass rate vs route features

```{python}
if not corr.empty:
    fa = corr[corr["outcome"] == "first_attempt_pass_rate"].head(20)
    if not fa.empty:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, max(4, len(fa) * 0.4 + 1)))
        colours = ["#e74c3c" if r < 0 else "#3498db" for r in fa["r"]]
        ax.barh(fa["feature"], fa["r"], color=colours)
        ax.axvline(0, color="black", linewidth=0.8)
        ax.set_xlabel("Pearson r")
        ax.set_title("Correlation with first-attempt pass rate\n(top 20 features by |r|)")
        ax.invert_yaxis()
        ax.grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)

        # Annotate significance
        for i, (_, row_) in enumerate(fa.iterrows()):
            if row_["sig"]:
                ax.text(row_["r"] + (0.01 if row_["r"] >= 0 else -0.01),
                        i, row_["sig"], va="center",
                        ha="left" if row_["r"] >= 0 else "right", fontsize=9)

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()

        print("\nTop correlates with first-attempt pass rate:")
        print(fa[["feature", "r", "p", "sig", "n"]].to_string(index=False))
```

### Road fault index

```{python}
if not corr.empty:
    for outcome_col in ["road_fault_index_sd", "adjusted_difficulty_sd",
                        "road_fault_index_minor"]:
        top = corr[corr["outcome"] == outcome_col].head(10)
        if top.empty:
            continue

        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, max(3, len(top) * 0.4 + 1)))
        colours = ["#e74c3c" if r < 0 else "#3498db" for r in top["r"]]
        ax.barh(top["feature"], top["r"], color=colours)
        ax.axvline(0, color="black", linewidth=0.8)
        ax.set_xlabel("Pearson r")
        label_map = {
            "road_fault_index_sd": "road fault index (SD rate)",
            "adjusted_difficulty_sd": "adjusted difficulty (road − aptitude, SD)",
            "road_fault_index_minor": "road fault index (minor rate)",
        }
        ax.set_title(f"Correlation with {label_map.get(outcome_col, outcome_col)}")
        ax.invert_yaxis()
        ax.grid(axis="x", alpha=0.2)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.show()
        break
```

### Junction-specific faults

```{python}
if not corr.empty:
    junction_outcomes = [c for c in corr["outcome"].unique()
                         if "junction" in c.lower()]
    for oc in junction_outcomes[:2]:
        top = corr[corr["outcome"] == oc].head(8)
        if top.empty:
            continue
        print(f"\n--- {oc} ---")
        print(top[["feature", "r", "p", "sig", "n"]].to_string(index=False))
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**Interpretation caution**

The correlation analysis has fewer than 20 test centres in most runs. With
this sample size, effect sizes need to be large (|r| > 0.5) to approach
significance, and findings should be treated as exploratory. The adjusted
difficulty index partially controls for candidate quality by subtracting
aptitude-linked fault rates, but does not fully account for regional
socioeconomic variation in candidate pool.
:::

---

## Summary: highest and lowest risk routes

```{python}
if not feats.empty and "risk_percentile_mean" in feats.columns:
    show_cols = [c for c in [
        "dtc_name", "n_routes", "risk_percentile_mean", "pct_high_risk",
        "pct_roundabout", "pct_high_speed", "pct_right_turns",
        "mean_speed_est",
    ] if c in feats.columns]

    sorted_all = feats.dropna(subset=["risk_percentile_mean"]).sort_values(
        "risk_percentile_mean", ascending=False
    )

    for c in ["risk_percentile_mean", "pct_high_risk", "pct_roundabout",
              "pct_high_speed", "pct_right_turns"]:
        if c in sorted_all and c.startswith("pct_"):
            sorted_all[c] = (sorted_all[c] * 100).round(1)
        elif c in sorted_all:
            sorted_all[c] = sorted_all[c].round(1)

    print("All centres ranked by mean route risk:\n")
    print(sorted_all[show_cols].to_string(index=False))
```
